data.title: LVMH is well-placed for Armani’s bespoke auction
data.description:
The late designer instructed heirs to sell 15% of his group, with scope to hit 70% after three years. LVMH, EssilorLuxottica and L’Oréal are suitors with first dibs. Bernard Arnault’s couture background and size put him in pole position for a prize worth at least 10 bln euros.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow Yawen Chen on Bluesky and LinkedIn.

CONTEXT NEWS

The will of late fashion designer Giorgio Armani instructs heirs to sell a 15% stake in Italian fashion house Armani within 18 months, and later transfer an additional 30% to 54.9% to the same buyer between three and five years after his death, Reuters reported on September 12 citing a copy of the will.

The will states that priority should be given to luxury giant LVMH, beauty heavyweight L'Oréal, eyewear leader EssilorLuxottica or other fashion and luxury companies with which Armani has commercial ties with.

As an alternative, an initial public listing should be pursued, the will added.

EssilorLuxottica said on September 12 it would carefully examine a possible investment in Armani, adding "we're proud of the consideration our group and his management received from Mr Armani".

Metals One Plc

Update re Increased Stake in Evolution Energy Minerals

ABOUT PETROTAL

PetroTal is a publicly traded, tri‐quoted (TSX: TAL, AIM: PTAL and OTCQX: PTALF) oil and gas development and production Company domiciled in Calgary, Alberta, focused on the development of oil assets in Peru.  PetroTal's flagship asset is its 100% working interest in Bretana oil field in Peru's Block 95 where oil production was initiated in June 2018.  In early 2022, PetroTal became the largest crude oil producer in Peru.  The Company's management team has significant experience in developing and exploring for oil in Peru and is led by a Board of Directors that is focused on safely and cost effectively developing the Bretana oil field. It is actively building new initiatives to champion community sensitive energy production, benefiting all stakeholders.

data.description:

----

Front and Center in This Week's IPOs: Individual Investors

Klarna, Gemini and others have set aside more shares for everyday buyers.

----

U.K. Economy Stagnated in July

Gross domestic product ground to a halt in July, matching consensus estimates, though the Bank of England was still expected to persist with rate cuts.

----

Firm Inflation, Soft Jobs Data Pull Fed in Opposing Directions

Consumer prices were up 2.9% in August from a year earlier, hotter than July's gain of 2.7%. The year-over-year reading was the highest since the start of 2025.

----

ECB has paused its rate-cutting cycle, likely cementing a role reversal with the Federal Reserve, which is expected to resume one next week

----

Cenovus Energy has sold its 50% interest in a U.S. refinery business to its joint venture partner Phillips 66 for $1.4 billion.

Sabadell Board Recommends Shareholders Don't Accept BBVA's $18 Billion Offer

OpenAI's Funding Challenges Loom Over Oracle, Broadcom Deal Spree

----

Adobe Boosts Fiscal-Year View as AI Spending Starts to Pay Off

----

Gemini's IPO rides the crypto craze to further the fortunes of the Winklevoss twins

----

RH Trims Outlook Amid Continued Tariff Uncertainty

----

Berkshire-Owned Talc Supplier Wins Appellate Ruling to Remain in Chapter 11

An appeals court kept defunct talc supplier Whittaker Clark & Daniels under chapter 11, ruling against a receiver from South Carolina who argued the bankruptcy case was improperly filed.

data.title: Camtek Announces Pricing of Upsized Private Offering of $425M of 0.00% Convertible Senior Notes Due 2030
The Board of Directors of VNV Global has resolved to repurchase own shares
data.description:
The Board of Directors of VNV Global AB (publ) (the “Company”) has resolved, by virtue of authorisation by the annual general meeting on 14 May 2025, to repurchase the Company’s own ordinary shares.

The purpose of the resolution on acquisition of own shares is to provide flexibility in relation to the Company’s possibilities to return capital to its shareholders, to improve the capital efficiency in the Company, and to prevent a NAV/share price discount in relation to the Company’s shares.

Acquisition of own ordinary shares shall take place on Nasdaq Stockholm in accordance with Nasdaq Stockholm’s Rulebook for Issuers. The acquisitions may be made on one or several occasions, however not longer than until the next annual general meeting. Ordinary shares may be acquired to the extent that the company’s holding of its own shares, on any occasion, does not exceed ten (10) per cent of the Company’s total shares. Acquisition of shares may only take place at a price within the price interval, on any occasion, recorded on Nasdaq Stockholm, which refers to the interval between the highest buying price and the lowest selling price. Any acquisition of own ordinary shares is also subject to the terms under the Company’s outstanding bonds.

As of the date of this press release, the Company holds no ordinary shares and the total number of shares amounts to 137,110,235 divided into six classes of shares as follows:

  • 130,978,236 ordinary shares, with one vote each.
  • 111,999 reclassifiable subordinated incentive shares of Class C-2022, with one vote each.
  • 1,710,000 reclassifiable subordinated incentive shares of Class C-2023, with one vote each.
  • 1,710,000 reclassifiable subordinated incentive shares of Class D-2023, with one vote each.
  • 1,300,000 reclassifiable subordinated incentive shares of Class C-2024, with one vote each.
  • 1,300,000 reclassifiable subordinated incentive shares of Class C-2025, with one vote each.

data.title: Tesla Stock Is on a Tear. Where It Goes Next. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

Tesla stock rose in early trading Friday as investors suddenly appeared more optimistic about the outlook. It's also possible traders are in control of the recent rally.

Shares of the electric-vehicle maker were up 0.3% at $367.80, while S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average futures were down 0.1% and 0.2%, respectively.

The move comes after Tesla stock added 6% on Thursday, helped in part by in-line inflation data that solidified investors' belief that rate cuts are coming. Auto companies like lower rates because cars are generally financed. Shares of General Motors and Ford Motor rose 2.4% and 3%, respectively, on Thursday as well.

Tesla shares notched their highest close since February on Thursday, leaving investors wondering what's coming next.

"Tesla broke out of a short-term [trading] pattern in August, which targeted a [trading] level near $384," said Will Tamplin, a senior technical analyst at Fairlead Strategies. "We think that level will be reached soon since short-term momentum is accelerating with [Thursday's] move."

He sees another 4% or 5% in the recent rally. Tamplin isn't making a fundamental call on Tesla stock. Technical analysts use stock charts and market history to gauge investors' sentiment and forecast where a stock can go over the short and medium term.

Traders use charts, too, and they like to buy things that look like they have more room to run.

Coming into Friday trading, Tesla stock was down about 9% so far this year, but up about 62% over the past 12 months. Investors have been balancing falling car sales with AI opportunities.

Tesla delivered about 721,000 cars in the first half of 2025, down 13% year over year. It also successfully launched a small self-driving cab service in Austin, Texas in June.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: India's Inflation Ticks Up as Expected, Snapping Cooling Streak
data.description:
By Fabiana Negrin Ochoa

India's consumer-price growth edged higher in August, snapping a nine-month streak of declines.

The consumer-price index rose 2.07% in August from a year earlier, up 46 basis points from July, government data showed Friday. The print was broadly in line with the 2.10% increase forecast in a Wall Street Journal poll of economists.

The pickup in headline and food inflation was driven by higher prices for vegetables, meat and fish, according to the release.

The uptick had been widely expected due to rising food costs and is unlikely to alter the central bank's policy stance after months of cooling inflation.

DBS economists said the Reserve Bank of India will likely weigh the country's strong gross domestic product growth in the past quarter against the softer inflation trend, and lean toward a pause at its next meeting.

At its last meeting, the Reserve Bank of India also held steady.

The RBI maintained its growth forecast for the financial year ending March 2026 at 6.5%, while lowering its inflation forecast to 3.1% from 3.7% on expectations of a more benign price outlook.

Capital Economics thinks inflation has bottomed out but will only gradually rise back to the RBI's 4% target.

"This gives the Reserve Bank [of India] scope to resume its loosening cycle to provide a bit more policy support in the face of punitive U.S. tariffs," said Shilan Shah, deputy chief emerging markets economist at Capital Economics.

Write to Fabiana Negrin Ochoa at fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com

data.title: India Inflation to Rise But at Very Gradual Pace — Market Talk
data.description: India's headline CPI rose for the first time in 10 months in August, but will take a long while yet to get back up to the RBI's 4% target, Capital Economics says. "This gives the Reserve Bank scope to resume its loosening cycle to provide a bit more policy support in the face of punitive U.S. tariffs," writes economist Shilan Shah. Inflation will keep gathering steam, but very gradually, he expects. Favorable weather will help keep a lid on food prices, while underlying pressure should stay in check as the economy cools after a very strong 1H. Given the 50% tariff on exports to the U.S., CE now thinks the RBI will add to its 100 bps of cuts so far this year. It projects another 50 bps of cuts, taking the repo rate down to 5.00% by end-2025. (fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1106 GMT - India's headline CPI rose for the first time in 10 months in August, but will take a long while yet to get back up to the RBI's 4% target, Capital Economics says. "This gives the Reserve Bank scope to resume its loosening cycle to provide a bit more policy support in the face of punitive U.S. tariffs," writes economist Shilan Shah. Inflation will keep gathering steam, but very gradually, he expects. Favorable weather will help keep a lid on food prices, while underlying pressure should stay in check as the economy cools after a very strong 1H. Given the 50% tariff on exports to the U.S., CE now thinks the RBI will add to its 100 bps of cuts so far this year. It projects another 50 bps of cuts, taking the repo rate down to 5.00% by end-2025. (fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

1057 GMT - The Bank of Japan is expected to keep its policy rate unchanged at 0.50% next week, HSBC economists say. The BOJ will likely move cautiously as it deals with domestic political uncertainty and seeks more evidence of economic resilience, Frederic Neumann and Justin Feng say. Policymakers are also waiting for more trade data in the wake of U.S. tariffs, as Japan's exporters face risks from a global slowdown. However, a strong 2Q GDP print and rising inflation back the case for the BOJ to raise rates by the end of year, HSBC says. It sees a 25bp October rate hike as probable but thinks the central bank could be pressured to delay if a more dovish leader succeeds outgoing PM Ishiba. (jason.chau@wsj.com)

1045 GMT - The risk premium on French government bonds, known as OATs, will stay elevated, says BlackRock Investment Institute's Roelof Salomons in a note. OATs have gone through renewed stress lately, especially in anticipation of the fall of previous Prime Minister Francois Bayrou's government in a confidence vote earlier this week. Still, concerns about French fiscal policy and debt shouldn't impact European Central Bank policy. "We do not expect the political turmoil in France to impact ECB decisions," Salomons says. BlackRock's view is that concerns about expansionary fiscal policy and mounting debt burdens are a global story and key market drivers over recent months. The 10-year French OAT-German Bund yield last trades at 78 basis points, according to LSEG data. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

1005 GMT - The cost of insuring euro credit against default stays close to 3.5-year low as risk appetite rises on prospects of the U.S. Federal Reserve cutting interest rates in September. "Investors are in a risk-on mood ahead of the U.S. interest rate decision next week," AJ Bell's Russ Mould says in a note. The iTraxx Europe Crossover index, which tracks euro junk bond credit default swaps, trades at 253 basis points, close to the low of 252bps touched on Thursday, S&P Global Market Intelligence data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

1001 GMT - Recent falls in very long-dated government bond yields are unlikely to last much longer, Capital Economics' Thomas Mathews says in a note. This week's 30-year U.S. Treasury auction suggested that demand is still decent but structural issues "are still lurking in the background," the head of markets for Asia Pacific says.U.S. financial accounts for the second quarter show that traditional buyers of long-dated U.S. Treasurys, such as defined-benefit pension funds and life insurers, didn't add at all to their holdings in the second quarter, he says. The 30-year Treasury yield is up 0.1 basis point at 4.659%, having hit 5.002% on Sept. 3, according to Tradeweb data. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0957 GMT - There may be some relief for U.S. Treasury investors to hear that President Trump isn't considering an outside maverick for the Federal Reserve job, which could help stabilize long-dated yields, BlueBay CIO Mark Dowding says in a note. BlueBay has always thought that the next leg of the steepening trade in U.S. Treasurys would be led by cash rates coming down rather than long-dated bonds selling off, he says. "We look for this to occur as the FOMC resumes its rate cutting cycle," he says. The Fed is expected to cut interest rates by 25 basis points next week, with small odds of a bigger 50-basis point cut, according to LSEG data. The 10-year Treasury yield rises 3.2 basis points to 4.042%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0930 GMT - Julius Baer expects the European Central Bank to cut interest rates in December, and acknowledges risks of less easing and an earlier conclusion to the monetary policy easing cycle, says economist David Meier in a note. "With the policy stance currently neutral at a deposit rate of 2.00%, and little need to shift to a looser stance, we stick to our expectation for the next ECB policy rate cut to come in December," he says. Expecting still two rate cuts in total, the risks to Julius Baer's view appear to be skewed rather towards further delays, the possibility of less policy easing, or that the monetary policy easing cycle is over altogether, Meier says. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0929 GMT - Weakness in the manufacturing sector held back U.K. GDP in July, economists at Investec Economics say in a note. U.K. manufacturing output dropped by 1.3% in July weighing on GDP growth which stagnated during the month. U.K. monthly GDP growth was flat in July, down from 0.4% growth in June.(miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0926 GMT - While U.K. growth stalled in July, the economy has shown remarkable resilience in the face of multiple external shocks in recent years, from volatile energy prices to trade tariffs, says Jonathan Marchant, fund manager at Mattioli Woods. The 0% growth in July comes after several forecast-beating economic indicators, including retail sales, purchasing managers' index and consumer confidence figures, he says in a note. However, it remains to be seen whether the economy can generate positive momentum heading into the government's budget due in November, where the range of potential outcomes remains wide, Woods says. After inflation was higher than expected and other indicators proving better than forecast, the Bank of England is almost certain to hold interest rates steady at next week's meeting, he says. (edward.frankl@wsj.com)

0925 GMT - Sterling could extend its losses as the latest U.K. gross domestic product data suggest the economy is in a fragile position ahead of November's autumn budget, XTB's Kathleen Brooks says in a note. The data showed the economy failed to grow in July. The "specter of stagflation," or weak growth combined with high inflation, "continues to hang over the U.K. economy," Brooks says. The Bank of England has sounded wary about cutting interest rates recently but the data could boost the market's rate-cut expectations and further undermine sterling, she says. Sterling falls 0.2% to $1.3543. The euro rises 0.2% to 0.8660 pounds. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0921 GMT - Japan's central bank will opt for a wait-and-see approach at its meeting next week, says Stefan Angrick at Moody's Analytics. Though better-than-expected GDP growth, sticky inflation and fresh yen depreciation keep a rate hike on the radar, policymakers will likely be cautious amid political uncertainty at home and abroad, the economist says. Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba's resignation scrambles the policy outlook, and the picture overseas isn't much better, with doubts over the U.S.-Japan trade deal persisting. Meanwhile, Japan's exports and industrial output are weakening, and consumer spending is faltering. "There isn't enough demand-led inflation to warrant a rate hike this month," Angrick writes. None of this is to say the BOJ can't hike, but given the shaky economic outlook, policymakers will likely want more clarity. (fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

0916 GMT - The U.K. economy was always likely to post flat activity growth in July after a strong 0.4% gain in June, Berenberg's Andrew Wishart says in a note. He puts more weight on the 1.4% annual growth, strongly outpacing neighbors Germany and France. But without a sudden improvement in productivity, GDP needs to slow for inflation to come down to the Bank of England's 2% target, he says. A soft patch is coming as government spending slows and companies suffer the impact of April's tax and minimum-wage increases. That might lead to stagnating employment and slower pay growth. Once this weakness gets inflation back on a downward trend, the BOE can step in with further rate cuts, he says. (edward.frankl@wsj.com)

data.title: IonQ Secures Regulatory Approval from the UK Investment Security Unit (ISU) for the Acquisition of Oxford Ionics
data.description:
COLLEGE PARK, Md.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--September 12, 2025--

IonQ , the leading commercial quantum computing and networking company, today announced that it has secured UK Investment Security Unit regulatory clearance for the acquisition of Oxford Ionics. All deal conditionality has been satisfied and the company looks forward to closing the deal in the near term.

About IonQ

IonQ, Inc. [NYSE: IONQ] is the leading commercial quantum computing and quantum networking company, delivering high-performance systems aimed at solving the world's most complex problems. IonQ's current generation quantum computers, IonQ Forte and IonQ Forte Enterprise, are the latest in a line of cutting-edge systems that have been helping customers and partners such as Amazon Web Services, AstraZeneca, and NVIDIA achieve 20x performance results.

The company is accelerating its technology roadmap and intends to deliver the world's most powerful quantum computers with 2 million qubits by 2030 to accelerate innovation in drug discovery, materials science, financial modeling, logistics, cybersecurity, and defense. IonQ's advancements in quantum networking also position the company as a leader in building the quantum internet.

The company's innovative technology and rapid growth were recognized in Newsweek's 2025 Excellence Index 1000, Forbes' 2025 Most Successful Mid-Cap Companies list, and Built In's 2025 100 Best Midsize Places to Work in Washington DC and Seattle, respectively. Available through all major cloud providers, IonQ is making quantum computing more accessible and impactful than ever before. Learn more at IonQ.com.

IonQ Forward-Looking Statements

This press release contains certain forward-looking statements within the meaning of Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, and Section 21E of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended. Some of the forward-looking statements can be identified by the use of forward-looking words. Statements that are not historical in nature, including but not limited to the terms "accelerate," "accelerates," "accelerating," "advance," "advancements," "building," "can, ""continue, " "deliver," "delivering," "develop," "expansion," "future," "growth," "intends," "plans," "supports," "toward," "will," and other similar expressions, are intended to identify forward-looking statements. These statements include those related to the IonQ's quantum computing capabilities and plans; IonQ's technology driving commercial quantum advantage in the future; the necessity, effectiveness, and future impacts of IonQ's offerings available today; and the scalability, fidelity, efficiency, viability, accessibility, effectiveness, importance, reliability, performance, speed, impact, practicality, feasibility, and commercial-readiness of IonQ's offerings. Forward-looking statements are predictions, projections, and other statements about future events that are based on current expectations and assumptions and, as a result, are subject to risks and uncertainties. Many factors could cause actual future events to differ materially from the forward-looking statements in this press release, including but not limited to: IonQ's ability to implement its technical roadmap; changes in the competitive industries in which IonQ operates, including development of competing technologies; IonQ's inability to attract and retain key personnel; IonQ's ability to deliver, and customers' ability to generate, value from IonQ's offerings; IonQ's inability to effectively integrate its acquisitions of Qubitekk, Inc., Lightsync Technologies, Inc., Capella Space Corporation, Oxford Ionics Limited, and ID Quantique, SA; changes in laws and regulations affecting IonQ's patents; and IonQ's ability to maintain or obtain patent protection for its products and technology, including with sufficient breadth to provide a competitive advantage. You should carefully consider the foregoing factors and the other risks and uncertainties disclosed in the Company's filings, including but not limited to those described in the "Risk Factors" section of IonQ's filings with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, including but not limited to the Company's most recent Annual Report on Form 10-K and reports on Form 10-Q. These filings identify and address other important risks and uncertainties that could cause actual events and results to differ materially from those contained in the forward-looking statements. Forward-looking statements speak only as of the date they are made. Readers are cautioned not to put undue reliance on forward-looking statements, and IonQ assumes no obligation and does not intend to update or revise these forward-looking statements, whether as a result of new information, future events, or otherwise. IonQ does not give any assurance that it will achieve its expectations.

data.title: MindWalk Holdings Corp expected to post a loss of 5 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• MindWalk Holdings Corp is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on September 15 for the period ending July 31 2025

  • The Austin Texas-based company is expected to report a 18.8% increase in revenue to C$6.25 million from C$5.26 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 2 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for MindWalk Holdings Corp is for a loss of 5 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for MindWalk Holdings Corp is $5.00, about 56.6% above its last closing price of $2.17

RNS Number : 1767Z abrdn Diversified Income and Growth 12 September 2025  

abrdn Holdings Limited announces the unaudited net asset values (NAVs) of the following investment companies as at close of business on 11 September 2025. Unless otherwise disclosed, the NAVs have been calculated in accordance with the recommendations of the Association of Investment Companies. In particular: (1) financial assets have been valued on a fair value basis using bid prices, or, if more appropriate, a last trade basis; (2) debt is valued at par and, where applicable, debt is also separately valued at market value (3) diluted NAVs are disclosed where applicable (for this purpose, treasury shares are excluded for the purposes of calculation); and (4) provisions for performance fees are included where applicable.
